evidence_contract.question: On feasible paired actual-SIR d18 rows, does low-rank preserve validity/comparability and show practical runtime evidence versus compiled streaming?
evidence_contract.baseline: Existing compiled streaming actual-SIR TF32/GPU route timed with --streaming-timing-source compiled_core.
evidence_contract.candidate: Existing TensorFlow low-rank coupling solver route.
evidence_contract.primary_support_screen: At least two adjacent paired rows with no hard vetoes, no paired comparability vetoes, same physical GPU UUID, warmups=1, repeats=3, and warm median streaming / low_rank >= 1.25.

paired_comparability_failures: filtered_mean_relative_l2 <= 0.20 passed, but filtered_mean_rms > 2.5; gate passes by relative alternative
filtered_variance_rms <= 25.0 passed, but filtered_variance_relative_l2 > 0.75; gate passes by RMS alternative

gate_assessment.paired_comparability: FAIL because log-likelihood paired agreement thresholds failed on the first required row
gate_assessment.warm_time_screen: FAIL because low-rank warm median was about 60.2x slower than compiled streaming on the first required row
gate_assessment.large_n_handoff: BLOCKED because P03 did not establish the required paired validity/comparability basis

nonclaims: No large-N executable-envelope claim for this actual-SIR lane
